patches.find: A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional (predicting precession acceleration, not rate) — but VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales, so a specific angular-acceleration value would imply a detectable changing precession rate that neither historical data nor modern geodesy records.
patches.replace: A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional — that under a τ = Iα framing, a specific angular-acceleration value is meaningful as the second derivative of the precession angle driven by luni-solar torques. The escape fails on magnitude: Earth’s observed secular acceleration of luni-solar precession is captured in the IAU 2006 P_03 model as the t² coefficient of the longitudinal expression (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s² — about twelve orders of magnitude smaller than the dome’s 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s². If the dome’s number were a real angular acceleration, integrating it over a single year would multiply Earth’s actual precession rate by a factor of ~2×10⁷, a runaway that VLBI and lunar laser ranging would have caught decades ago. The dome’s own page text frames this as a precession rate matching the 25,772-year cycle (a velocity-class quantity, the same type as the IAU 2006 value), so the acceleration reading is a defender retreat the published claim does not actually make.
patches.rationale: Closes the tau=Ialpha defender retreat (WIN-021.c3 moderate hole, ISS-1851) with IAU 2006 P_03 magnitude bound (~10^-24 vs dome 4.87e-12 rad/s^2, twelve OOM). Flags runaway consequence and dome-side framing mismatch. EXP-326 analyst work. Verdict unchanged (Misleading).

patches.find: Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well. The dome's 'aetheric vortex' label adds nothing — no distinct prediction, no mechanism, no derivation.
patches.replace: Both models can be drawn through the same NOAA points, but the dome's specific claimed rate (0.0466 deg/yr) cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric. The aetheric vortex label adds nothing — no distinct prediction, no mechanism, no derivation.
patches.rationale: WIN-006.c4 (moderate): tldr_verdict first sentence concedes models fit equally well, but detail_verdict_text (ISS-1912 patch) now documents the rate is not reproducible. Aligning tldr with patched verdict prose.

patches.find: The eight-gravimeter network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites.
patches.replace: The Chinese SG network — described by the 2013 CJG record as approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six sites — sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites.
patches.rationale: WIN-014.c10 (minor): tldr_evidence sentence 3 says "eight-gravimeter network" as a definite count, but detail_evidence (EXP-323/ISS-1829 hedge) now says "approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six sites". Soften tldr to match.
